name: Chicken Breasts with Apples and Cider Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, reduce the cider to 1/2 cup.
2. Whisk in mustard, cream, and canned milk. Reduce to about 2 cups over medium-high heat or until the sauce thickens. Add the seasoning, set aside to cool.
3. Dredge chicken in combined flour, salt, and black pepper.
4. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add chicken and sauté on one side for about 5 minutes.
5. Turn and sauté for 3-5 more minutes or until just slightly undone. Remove chicken; keep warm.
6. Core and slice apples into 1/4-inch slices. Add remaining oil to the pan. Add apple slices and saute for 3-5 minutes or until just tender. Remove apples.
7. Add cider cream sauce to the pan and heat while scraping up any browned bits from the pan bottom. Return chicken to warm sauce for one minute, then top with apple rings and sauce.
8. Heat for one more minute and serve. Can be held in a slack oven or very low heat for about 10-15 minutes.
